summary_text: 1. [assets/hooks/user-prompt-submit-hook.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/user-prompt-submit-hook.sh) Add a new runtime stage right after Layer 1C that auto-injects a compact `philosophy.md` memo only for writing/author-memory prompts (with opt-out tags like `#no-philosophy`), so philosophy usage happens on real prompt flow instead of policy-only mention. 2. [assets/hooks/vault-context-inject.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/vault-context-inject.mjs) Implement “philosophy-first” behavior for writing intents: read `~/.claude/docs/philosophy.md` first, emit a short worldview memo, then optionally add Obsidian snippets; this prevents raw-note drift and materially raises philosophy usage in author-memory retrieval. 3. [assets/docs/philosophy.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/philosophy.md) Add a machine-readable `Runtime Memo` section (fixed headings + short bullets) explicitly designed for hook extraction, so runtime code can reliably inject consistent worldview context without brittle parsing. 4. [assets/docs/kernel-author-memory-router.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/kernel-author-memory-router.md), [assets/docs/author-memory-pack.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/author-memory-pack.md) Update contracts/examples to reflect the new runtime order (`philosophy.md` before Obsidian for writing tasks), explicit opt-outs, and expected injected payload shape; this keeps author-memory and writing flows aligned with actual runtime behavior. 5. [.github/workflows/test-orchestrator.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.github/workflows/test-orchestrator.yml) Extend CI scope to run a tiny hook smoke test for philosophy injection paths (writing prompt, non-writing prompt, opt-out), so the runtime adoption change does not regress silently.
